FDA Approves New Treatment for Alzheimer's Disease

January 10, 2024

FR Doc. 2024-00123

RIN 2024-ABCD

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved a new treatment for Alzheimer's disease. This approval marks a significant advancement in the treatment options available for patients with early-stage Alzheimer's disease.

The approval is based on clinical trial data demonstrating the drug's ability to reduce amyloid-beta plaques in the brain, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.

This press release is issued by the FDA's Center for Drug Evaluation and Research.
